title: Present perfect negatives — haven't and hasn't
description: Form and use negative present perfect sentences to talk about things that have not happened.

content.title: Forming the negative — haven't and hasn't
content.body:
To make a NEGATIVE present perfect sentence, add NOT after have/has:

have not = HAVEN'T
has not = HASN'T

Structure:
I / You / We / They + HAVEN'T + past participle
He / She / It + HASN'T + past participle

Examples:
• 'I haven't eaten lunch yet.'
• 'She hasn't called back.'
• 'We haven't seen that film.'
• 'He hasn't finished the report.'
• 'They haven't arrived yet.'

Full forms (more formal/emphatic):
• 'I have not received any reply.'
• 'She has not submitted the form.'
content.grammar_note:
haven't = have not (I/you/we/they)
hasn't = has not (he/she/it)
Full forms used for emphasis or formal writing

content.title: Common contexts for negative present perfect
content.body:
We use the negative present perfect in many everyday situations:

Tasks not done:
• 'I haven't finished the assignment.'
• 'She hasn't booked the tickets yet.'

Experiences not had:
• 'I haven't been to North India.'
• 'He hasn't tried Korean food.'

Expected things that didn't happen:
• 'They haven't replied to our email.'
• 'The doctor hasn't called back yet.'

Useful combinations:
• haven't + yet = expected but not done
• still + haven't = stronger expectation
• haven't + ever = never in my life
content.grammar_note:
haven't yet = expected soon
still haven't = been waiting / frustrated
haven't ever = same as never

content.title: Vocabulary — things not done or experienced

vocabulary.meaning: to succeed in doing something difficult
vocabulary.example: I haven't managed to fix it yet.

vocabulary.meaning: to find time to do something
vocabulary.example: I haven't got round to calling her yet.

vocabulary.meaning: to make the effort to do something

vocabulary.example: She hasn't attempted the last question.

vocabulary.meaning: to handle a situation or task
vocabulary.example: I haven't dealt with those emails yet.
He hasn't got back to me yet.

sentences: I haven't finished the report yet — give me an hour.
She hasn't called back — I'm still waiting.
We haven't been to Rajasthan — it's on our list.
He hasn't got back to me about the meeting time.
They haven't replied to the proposal yet.

content.question: Choose the correct negative form: 'She ___ the email.'
content.explanation: She = HASN'T + past participle (sent). Haven't is for I/you/we/they.

content.question: Which sentence is CORRECT?
options: We have not finishing the project.
We not have finished the project.
content.explanation: We = HAVEN'T + past participle. 'Hasn't' is wrong for we; -ing form is wrong.

content.question: Ravi says: 'I ___  to Ravi yet — I keep forgetting.' Which fits?
content.explanation: I = HAVEN'T + past participle (spoken). The 'yet' confirms present perfect is needed.

content.question: Which sentence correctly uses the FULL FORM for emphasis?
options: She not has received any confirmation.
She has not received any confirmation.
She hasn't not received any confirmation.
She have not received any confirmation.
content.correct: She has not received any confirmation.
content.explanation: Full form: HAS + NOT (not 'not has'). She has not = formal and emphatic.

content.instruction: Fill in with HAVEN'T or HASN'T + the past participle.

sentences.text: I ___ ___ (start) the assignment — I'll begin tonight.
We ___ ___ (decide) on a venue for the wedding.

content.instruction: Make these positive sentences negative using haven't or hasn't.

sentences.text: I have finished the chapter. → I ___ ___ the chapter.
She has tried sushi. → She ___ ___ sushi.
They have confirmed the booking. → They ___ ___ the booking.
He has spoken to the manager. → He ___ ___ to the manager.

content.instruction: Arrange into a correct negative sentence.
content.answer: We haven't received the results yet.

content.instruction: Arrange into a correct negative sentence.
content.answer: She hasn't submitted the form yet.

content.instruction: Match the subject to the correct negative form.

content.title: Things on the to-do list — Chennai household
content.setting: Kavya and her husband Arjun are reviewing their weekend to-do list. Many things haven't been done yet.

dialogue.speaker: Arjun
dialogue.text: Kavya, have you paid the electricity bill yet?

dialogue.speaker: Kavya
dialogue.text: No — I haven't paid it yet. I'll do it online right now.

dialogue.speaker: Arjun
dialogue.text: Okay. And have you called your mother back? She messaged me this morning.

dialogue.speaker: Kavya
dialogue.text: I know — I haven't got round to it yet. I've been busy all morning. Has the courier arrived?

dialogue.speaker: Arjun
dialogue.text: No, he hasn't come yet. I've been waiting since 10 AM.

dialogue.speaker: Kavya
dialogue.text: Strange — they haven't sent a tracking update either. Let me check the website.

dialogue.speaker: Arjun
dialogue.text: Good idea. By the way — I haven't booked the restaurant for Saturday yet. Should I do it now?

dialogue.speaker: Kavya
dialogue.text: Yes please — I haven't checked if they're open on Saturdays though. Call and confirm first.

dialogue.speaker: Arjun
dialogue.text: Sure. And have you taken the car for service? The garage said it was overdue.

dialogue.speaker: Kavya
dialogue.text: I haven't had time — I'll go next week. There's too much happening this weekend.

dialogue.speaker: Arjun
dialogue.text: Fair enough. Oh — have the kids finished their homework?

dialogue.speaker: Kavya
dialogue.text: Riya hasn't started hers yet but Vikram has finished. I'll speak to Riya after lunch.

dialogue.speaker: Arjun
dialogue.text: Great. Right — let's make a proper list so we don't forget anything else we haven't dealt with!

comprehension_questions.question: What has Kavya not done yet that Arjun's mother is waiting for?
Which child hasn't started their homework yet?
What hasn't Arjun done for Saturday yet?

content.instruction: Find and correct the negative present perfect mistake.

sentences.explanation: She = HASN'T (not haven't). Haven't is for I/you/we/they.

sentences.wrong: I have not receiving the parcel yet.
sentences.correct: I have not received the parcel yet.
sentences.explanation: After have/has (not), use the PAST PARTICIPLE — not the -ing form.

sentences.wrong: They hasn't replied to our email.
sentences.correct: They haven't replied to our email.
sentences.explanation: They = HAVEN'T (not hasn't). Hasn't is only for he/she/it.

content.scenario: Your colleague asks if you've sent the weekly update to the whole team. You forgot. What do you say honestly?
options: I haven't sent it yet — I'll do it right now, sorry!
I didn't send it last week.
content.correct: I haven't sent it yet — I'll do it right now, sorry!
content.explanation: Honest, present perfect negative + immediate action plan. Natural and professional.

content.instruction: Think of 6 things you HAVEN'T done yet this week — tasks, goals, or experiences. Explain each one briefly.
content.prompt: Share 6 things you haven't done yet this week and why.
content.example_response: There are quite a few things I haven't managed this week! I haven't replied to some important messages because my schedule has been completely packed. I haven't exercised at all — I planned to go jogging three times but the weather has not been great. I haven't cooked a proper meal even once — I've been surviving on takeaway food which I know isn't ideal. I haven't called my grandparents yet, which I feel guilty about because they always love to hear from me. I haven't read the news at all this week — I usually try to stay updated but I simply haven't had time. And I haven't finished the online module I started last Sunday — I've watched three lessons but I haven't completed the final quiz yet. Hopefully the weekend will give me time to catch up on all of these!

content.instruction: Talk about 3 places in India or the world that you haven't visited yet but really want to. Explain why you haven't gone and why you want to go.
content.prompt: Describe 3 places you haven't been to yet. Use haven't/hasn't been naturally.
content.example_response: There are three places I haven't visited yet that are really on my list. The first is Ladakh — I haven't been there because it requires good planning and the right season, and I haven't managed to get both of those things right at the same time. But the photographs I have seen are extraordinary — the landscapes look like another planet. The second is Hampi in Karnataka — surprisingly I haven't been there even though it's not too far from Chennai. I haven't found the right travel companion for it because it's the kind of place that deserves a slow, curious exploration. And the third is Japan. I haven't been abroad much and Japan has been my dream destination for years. The culture, the food, the architecture — I haven't experienced anything quite like what people describe. I haven't saved enough for it yet either, but I'm working on it!

content.instruction: Complete this project status email using haven't or hasn't + past participle.
content.text:
Subject: Project Status Update — Week 3

Hi team,

Here is a quick update on where things stand. I ___ (1) finalised the budget yet — I'm waiting for the finance team's approval. Sonal ___ (2) submitted her section of the report yet but she confirmed it'll be ready by Thursday. We ___ (3) scheduled the client review meeting — could someone pick a date this week? The design team ___ (4) sent the updated mockups yet, which is holding up the development side. I ___ (5) had a chance to review the competitor analysis either — I'll try to do that tomorrow. Arjun, you ___ (6) shared the access credentials with the new team members yet — could you do that today please? Overall we ___ (7) fallen behind seriously but there are a few things we need to action quickly. Let's catch up on Friday.

Thanks,
Kavya

content.title: Lesson 6 complete — Present perfect negatives
key_points: HAVEN'T = have not — used with I/you/we/they
HASN'T = has not — used with he/she/it
Full forms (have not / has not) for emphasis or formal writing
Common combos: haven't + yet / still + haven't / haven't + ever
Common mistake: using hasn't with they/we — always use haven't
content.next_lesson: Present perfect questions — Have you...? Has she...?
